levels.label: Sales-Dependent
levels.copy: The Product-Led Growth Readiness pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Assisted
levels.copy: Some Product-Led Growth Readiness practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Product-Enabled
levels.copy: Core Product-Led Growth Readiness practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Product-Led
levels.copy: Product-Led Growth Readiness practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Compounding PLG
levels.copy: Product-Led Growth Readiness is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.time-to-value: Define and instrument the first-value event, then remove the largest step before it.
recommendations.activation-and-self-service: Design a segment-specific activation path and progressively disclose complexity.
recommendations.product-signals-and-data: Govern the event taxonomy and validate PQL/PQA signals against durable revenue outcomes.
recommendations.expansion-and-virality: Model one collaboration or sharing loop and align upgrade triggers with realised value.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
